data.to: Hepatology Department, Milton Keynes University Hospital
data.from: Dr. M. Torres, Central MK Medical Group
data.reason_for_referral: Isolated unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia found on routine bloods during university exam stress period. Slight yellowing of eyes noted on examination. All other LFTs entirely normal.

data.chief_complaint: University student. Noticed slight yellowing of eyes during exam period. Otherwise well. No pain, no systemic symptoms.
data.hpi: 22M, healthy university student. Reports slight yellowing of eyes noticed by flatmate. Currently under exam stress, not sleeping well, skipping meals. No abdominal pain, no dark urine, no pale stools, no weight loss, no fever.

physical_examination.general: Slim, well-appearing young male. Mildly icteric sclera.
physical_examination.skin: Mild scleral icterus. No skin jaundice. No rashes.

data.assessment: Isolated hyperbilirubinemia with completely normal liver enzymes. Classic presentation for Gilbert syndrome — triggered by fasting/stress. Refer for confirmation and reassurance.

sections.title: Pre-Consultation Questionnaire
qa: Slight yellowing of eyes noticed during exam stress. No pain. No other symptoms.
About 2 units per week. Occasional beer with mates.
Dad says he had something similar as a teenager — yellow eyes but nothing wrong
